data.title: WEED, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
WEED, Inc., a company focused on developing cannabis-derived compounds for treating human diseases and animal ailments, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, shedding light on its current challenges and future plans in the cannabis and hemp industry.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: $0. The company has not had any revenues since inception and plans to enter the hemp and infused beverage industry once sufficient funding is secured.

Net Operating Loss: $(600,805). The net operating loss increased by $198,275 compared to the same period last year, primarily due to increases in general and administrative expenses.

Net Loss: $(622,018). The comprehensive net loss increased by $242,395 compared to the same period last year, driven by higher operating expenses.

Net Loss Per Share - Basic and Fully Diluted: $(0.005). This reflects the increased net loss over a larger number of shares outstanding compared to the previous year.

Business Highlights

Business Focus: WEED, Inc. is focused on developing and applying cannabis-derived compounds for treating human diseases and animal ailments. The company is also planning to enter the hemp and infused beverage industry through its newly acquired property in New York.

Geographical Performance: The company operates primarily within the United States, with minimal operations in Australia. WEED Australia Ltd. was deregistered effective November 19, 2025, which did not have a material impact on the company's consolidated financial statements.

New Product Launches: The company acquired Hempirical Genetics, LLC, which includes over 200 high THC strains and 30+ CBD & CBG strains. This acquisition is expected to enhance the company's product offerings in the cannabis sector.

Future Outlook: WEED, Inc. plans to become a 'Seed-to-Sale' company, providing infrastructure, financial solutions, and real estate options in the emerging cannabis market. The company is actively pursuing new products and services to begin generating revenues and is seeking additional sources of capital to fund short-term operations.

Operational Challenges: The company has not generated any revenue since inception and continues to incur expenses related to research and development, general and administrative activities, and other operational costs. Management is actively seeking additional financing to support operations and future growth.

Research and Development: The company is focused on completing a genetic blueprint of the Cannabis plant genus through its subsidiary, Sangre AT, LLC. This includes a planned five-year Cannabis Genomic Study, which requires additional funding to proceed.

Strategic Initiatives: WEED, Inc. is exploring several business opportunities in the cannabis and hemp field, including indoor and outdoor cultivation, product development, and manufacturing of both Pharma and non-Pharma products. The company aims to leverage its property in New York to access the hemp and infused beverage markets.

SEC Filing: WEED, INC. [ BUDZ ] - 10-Q - Dec. 23, 2025

data.title: Strathcona Shares Fall on C$10 Special Distribution, Debt-Facility Expansion
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Strathcona Resources shares fell after the company said it paid out its special distribution to shareholders, increased its debt facility and sold off all its publicly traded investments.

Shares traded in Toronto 25% lower at 29.54 Canadian dollars on Tuesday.

The Canadian oil-and-gas producer said late Monday that it completed its special distribution of C$10 a share by way of a plan of arrangement and expects shareholders to receive their distribution after Dec. 22.

The company has also redeemed all of its outstanding US$500 million bonds holding a rate of 6.875% due next year and has expanded its credit facility to C$3.49 billion from C$3.255 billion.

The updated credit agreement includes a C$265 million accordion feature to expand the credit to up to C$3.755 billion.

Additionally, Strathcona sold off all of its marketable-security portfolio over November and December for C$1.39 billion in proceeds, reflecting a gain of C$101 million compared with Sept. 30. Marketable securities can include assets as stocks, bonds, or other liquid financial assets.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Strategy Raises Cash Reserves to $2.19 Billion, Skips Bitcoin Purchases in Latest Filing
data.description:
Michael Saylor's Strategy disclosed on Monday that it increased the US dollar cash reserves it maintains to support dividend payments on its preferred stock and interest tied to outstanding debt, while opting not to add to its bitcoin position during the latest reporting window. The filing shows no bitcoin purchases between Dec. 15 and Dec. 21, 2025, suggesting the company is prioritizing liquidity management over additional accumulation in the near term.

The disclosure shows Strategy held $2.19 billion in US dollar reserves as of Dec. 21, 2025, up from $1.44 billion as of Dec. 1. The rise in cash balances could be interpreted as a move to reinforce its ability to meet dividend and debt obligations, particularly as the company continues to manage a capital structure that includes preferred equity and outstanding borrowings.

With no recent buying activity, Strategy's aggregate bitcoin holdings remain at 671,268 BTC, acquired for a total purchase price of $50.3 billion, or an average of $74,972 per bitcoin. For investors, the update possibly highlights a tactical pause, with the company maintaining its sizable bitcoin exposure while temporarily emphasizing balance-sheet flexibility and financial commitments.

data.title: First BanCorp to Announce 4Q 2025 Results on January 27, 2026
data.description:
SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico--(BUSINESS WIRE)--December 23, 2025--

First BanCorp (the "Corporation") , the bank holding company for FirstBank Puerto Rico, announced today that it expects to report its financial results for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025, before the market opens on January 27, 2026.

First BanCorp will hold a conference call and live webcast to discuss the financial results at 10:00am Eastern Time on Tuesday, January 27, 2026. The call and webcast will be broadcast live over the internet and can be accessed through the Corporation's investor relations website: fbpinvestor.com.

Listeners are recommended to go to the website at least 15 minutes prior to the call to download and install any necessary software. The call may also be accessed through a dial-in telephone number 833-470-1428 or 646-844-6383. The participant access code is 822609. Following the webcast presentation, a question-and-answer session will be made available to research analysts and institutional investors.

A telephone replay will be available until February 26, 2026, at 866-813-9403, with access code 830837. A replay of the webcast will be archived on First BanCorp's website until January 27, 2027.

About First BanCorp

First BanCorp is the parent corporation of FirstBank Puerto Rico, a state-chartered commercial bank with operations in Puerto Rico, the U.S. and British Virgin Islands and Florida, and of FirstBank Insurance Agency, LLC.

First BanCorp's shares of common stock trade on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "FBP."

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251223326522/en/

CONTACT: First BanCorp

Ramón Rodríguez

Senior Vice President

Corporate Strategy and Investor Relations

787-729-8200 x82179

ramon.rodriguez@firstbankpr.com

data.title: Canada Budget Deficit Widens in October From Year Ago
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canada's budget deficit widened in October, as expenditures slowed but still outstripped tepid revenue growth.

The budget deficit for October increased over 50% from year-earlier levels to 2.28 billion Canadian dollars, or the equivalent of $1.66 billion, according to the Department of Finance's monthly fiscal monitor publication. After the first seven months of the 2025-26 fiscal year, Canada's federal government ran a deficit of C$18.37 billion, versus C$14.50 billion in the comparable year-ago period.

Total expenditures on government programs and administration climbed 3.1% in October from a year ago, representing a deceleration from 4.6% growth in the prior month. The finance ministry said public-debt charges rose 2.1% in October, thereby bringing total expenses in October to C$43.82 billion, or up 3.2% from a year ago.

On the revenue side, tax receipts climbed 4.1% in October from a year ago, on solid gains in corporate and sales-tax proceeds. But the end of the consumer carbon tax, unveiled in March, led to a nearly C$1 billion decline from a year ago. As a result, total revenue in October rose 1.4% to C$41.54 billion.

The budget deficit for the current fiscal year is forecast to hit C$78.30 billion, or 2.5% of the country's gross domestic product. Excluding the pandemic period, the projected 2025-26 deficit would represent one of the biggest shortfalls, as a share of the economy, since the 2008-09 global financial crisis.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: U.S. Home Prices Tick Higher in November — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. home prices climbed 0.2% month-over-month in November, Redfin says, slightly smaller than October's 0.3% monthly increase. Prices rose 2.6% from a year earlier, a slightly slower growth rate than October's 2.9% year-over-year increase. Home-price growth has been slowing since the beginning of the year. Home-price growth has slowed this year on a year-over-year basis because many would-be homebuyers have pulled back. Elevated mortgage rates and widespread economic uncertainty have pushed buyers to the sidelines. But prices are still rising, not falling, because many prospective home sellers have started pulling back, too. Home prices fell in 11 major U.S. metros month-over-month on a seasonally adjusted basis in November. The biggest declines were in Charlotte, N.C.; Austin, Texas; and Cincinnati. The biggest increases were in Pittsburgh; Montgomery County, Pa.; and Chicago. (chris.wack@wsj.com)
